title: The Case of the Missing Waterhole
synopsis: When the animals' favorite waterhole mysteriously disappears, young detective Lily teams up with her animal friends to solve the case and discover what really happened to their precious water source.

pages.text: Lily loved exploring the African savanna with her family. She had long curly auburn hair that bounced as she walked, and her green eyes sparkled with curiosity. Today felt special - the golden grass swayed in the warm breeze, and the orange sun painted the sky like a beautiful picture.

illustration.description: A gouache painting with thick brushstrokes showing Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes walking through golden savanna grass under an orange sunset sky, warm colors dominating the scene
illustration.alt: Lily exploring the African savanna at sunset

pages.text: But something was wrong. The animals looked worried. Zara the zebra trotted up to Lily with tears in her big brown eyes. 'Our waterhole is gone!' she whinnied. 'We went there this morning, and it had completely disappeared!'

illustration.description: Gouache painting with bold confident strokes showing a sad zebra talking to Lily who has long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes, set against warm savanna colors with textured painterly golden grass
illustration.alt: Zara the zebra telling Lily about the missing waterhole

pages.text: Lily's detective instincts kicked in right away. She had solved mysteries before! 'Don't worry, Zara,' she said confidently. 'We'll find out what happened to your waterhole. Every good mystery has clues, and I'm going to find them all!'

illustration.description: Expressive gouache painting showing determined Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes pointing forward like a detective, surrounded by various worried savanna animals, painted with thick brushstrokes in warm orange and golden tones
illustration.alt: Lily promising to solve the mystery of the missing waterhole

pages.text: Lily and Zara walked to where the waterhole used to be. Instead of sparkling blue water, they found only dry, cracked earth. But Lily noticed something important - there were strange tracks leading away from the empty spot.

illustration.description: Gouache painting with textured painterly technique showing Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes and Zara examining cracked, dry ground with mysterious tracks, painted in warm savanna colors with thick brushstrokes
illustration.alt: Lily and Zara investigating the dry waterhole site

pages.text: The tracks were huge and round, bigger than elephant footprints! They led up a small hill covered in golden grass. 'These tracks look like they were made by something with wheels,' Lily observed, kneeling down to get a closer look.

illustration.description: Bold gouache painting showing Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes crouching to examine large wheel tracks in the dirt, golden grass hill in background, painted with confident strokes in orange sunset colors
illustration.alt: Lily examining mysterious wheel tracks on the ground

pages.text: More animals joined their investigation. Kesi the lion cub, Jengo the giraffe, and Penda the elephant all wanted to help solve the mystery. Together, they followed the strange tracks up the hill, getting closer to the truth with each step.

illustration.description: Expressive gouache painting with thick brushstrokes showing Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes leading a group of various savanna animals up a golden grass hill, warm colors and textured painterly style throughout
illustration.alt: Lily and animal friends following tracks up the hill

pages.text: At the top of the hill, they made an amazing discovery! There was their missing waterhole - but it wasn't missing at all. It had been moved! A big water truck sat nearby, and workers were digging a new, bigger waterhole.

illustration.description: Gouache painting with bold confident strokes showing Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes and animal friends looking at construction workers and a water truck near a new waterhole, painted in warm savanna colors
illustration.alt: The group discovering the waterhole construction site

pages.text: A kind worker named Mr. Banda saw the confused animals and smiled. 'We're sorry we worried you!' he called out. 'We moved your water to build you a much bigger, cleaner waterhole. It will be ready by tonight, and there will be enough water for everyone!'

illustration.description: Textured painterly gouache showing friendly worker speaking to Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes and excited animals, painted with thick brushstrokes in golden grass and orange sunset tones
illustration.alt: Mr. Banda explaining the waterhole project to Lily and the animals

pages.text: The mystery was solved! The waterhole hadn't disappeared - it was getting an upgrade. All the animals cheered and danced around Lily. She felt proud that she had helped her friends discover the truth behind the missing waterhole mystery.

illustration.description: Joyful gouache painting with expressive animals dancing around celebrating Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes, painted with thick brushstrokes in warm savanna colors under an orange sunset sky
illustration.alt: All the animals celebrating around Lily after solving the mystery

pages.text: That evening, all the animals gathered around their beautiful new waterhole. It was three times bigger than the old one, with crystal clear water that reflected the orange sunset sky. Everyone had plenty of room to drink and play.

illustration.description: Gorgeous gouache painting showing the new large waterhole reflecting orange sunset colors, with Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes and various savanna animals gathered around, painted with thick confident brushstrokes
illustration.alt: The animals enjoying their new, larger waterhole at sunset

pages.text: Zara splashed happily in the shallow end while Jengo bent his long neck down to drink. Penda sprayed water over her back with her trunk, and Kesi pounced at the ripples in the water. Everyone was having so much fun!

illustration.description: Expressive gouache painting with textured painterly technique showing animals playing in water - zebra splashing, giraffe drinking, elephant spraying, lion cub pouncing, while Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes watches happily
illustration.alt: Various animals playing and drinking at the new waterhole

pages.text: As the stars began to twinkle above, Lily smiled at her animal friends. She had learned that sometimes what seems like a big problem can actually be something wonderful in disguise. The best mysteries, she decided, are the ones with happy endings.

illustration.description: Peaceful gouache painting with thick brushstrokes showing Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, green eyes sitting contentedly with animal friends under a starry sky by the waterhole, warm golden and orange tones throughout
illustration.alt: Lily and friends under the stars by their new waterhole

metadata.illustrationStyle: gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes

questions.question: What was missing at the beginning of the story?
questions.explanation: The animals were worried because their waterhole had disappeared from its usual location.

questions.question: What kind of tracks did Lily find?
questions.explanation: Lily discovered large, round wheel tracks that led up the hill to where the construction was happening.

questions.question: Who told them about the new waterhole?
questions.explanation: Mr. Banda, the kind construction worker, explained that they were building a bigger, better waterhole.

questions.question: How was the new waterhole different from the old one?
questions.explanation: The new waterhole was three times bigger than the old one, with plenty of room for all the animals.

questions.question: What did Lily learn from solving this mystery?
choices: Sometimes problems can be good things in disguise
questions.explanation: Lily learned that what seemed like a big problem (the missing waterhole) was actually something wonderful - a bigger, better waterhole being built for the animals.
